**Subjective**
**Chief Complaint**
Dedicated medication review visit

**History of Present Illness**
Dorothy is an 82-year-old woman who came in for a dedicated medication review visit. I had set aside extra time specifically for this because when I looked at her chart, she had fourteen active prescriptions, and I wanted to go through every single one with her. She brought all her bottles in a bag — her daughter had organized them — and we spread them out and went through them together.

**Review of Systems**
Constitutional: [Fever, chills, fatigue, weight changes]
HEENT: [Vision, hearing, throat, nasal symptoms]
Cardiovascular: [Chest pain, palpitations, edema]
Respiratory: [Shortness of breath, cough, wheezing]
Gastrointestinal: [Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal pain]
Genitourinary: [Urinary symptoms, menstrual history]
Musculoskeletal: [Joint pain, muscle weakness, mobility issues]
Neurological: [Headache, dizziness, numbness, weakness]
Psychiatric: [Mood, anxiety, sleep]
Skin: [Rashes, lesions, wounds]

**Past Medical History**
[Chronic conditions and prior diagnoses]
(If not documented, state "Not documented")

**Medications**
[Current medications with dosages]
(If not documented, state "Not documented")

**Allergies**
[Drug allergies and reactions]
(If not documented, state "No known drug allergies" or "Not documented")

**Social History**
Tobacco: [Usage details]
Alcohol: [Usage details]
Recreational drugs: [Usage details]
Occupation: [Job details]
(Only include items that were mentioned)

[Relevant family medical history]

**Physical Examination**
**Vital Signs:**

HR: [Heart rate]
